name: Holy Tofu Mole!
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup chicken or vegetable stock (or water)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Remove the loose, papery skin from garlic and cut it in half crosswise.
3. Rub the cut surfaces with olive oil. Place garlic in a foil pouch and bake for 35-40 minutes until soft. Let cool enough to handle before squeezing out the cloves.
4. Remove stems and seeds from chilies. Roast under the broiler, turning frequently, until skin is evenly blistered and slightly charred. Place chilies on a separate plate. Remove skins and rinse.
5. Toast pumpkin seeds in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat, shaking the pan often, until they puff but do not brown. Transfer to a small bowl and let cool.
6. Grind pumpkin seeds to a fine meal using a food processor or blender.
7. Add roasted garlic, chilies, tomatillos, peppers, cilantro, tamari, and stock to the seed mixture. Puree until smooth.
8. Heat the sauce in a medium skillet and add stock. Simmer for 10 minutes.
9. Slice tofu into 1/2-inch thick pieces. Brush with tamari and grill over hot coals or broil in oven for 5 minutes per side.
10. Serve grilled tofu on top of a bed of rice, topped with mole sauce.
Calories per serving: 497
Grams of fat: 17
Percent fat calories: 32
Cholesterol: 0 mg
Grams fiber: 8.4
